# qbittorrent-exporter

![Version: 1.0.0](https://img.shields.io/badge/Version-1.0.0-informational?style=flat-square) ![Type: application](https://img.shields.io/badge/Type-application-informational?style=flat-square) ![AppVersion: v1.3.5](https://img.shields.io/badge/AppVersion-v1.3.5-informational?style=flat-square)
A Helm chart to export Prometheus metrics for qbittorrent.

A Helm Chart to deploy a Prometheus [exporter](https://github.com/caseyscarborough/qbittorrent-exporter) for qBittorrent. Based on the `caseyscarborough/qbittorrent-exporter` image.

## Add Chart Repo
```bash
helm repo add homeylab https://homeylab.github.io/helm-charts/
# update the chart, this can also be run to pull new versions of the chart for upgrades
helm repo update homeylab
```

## Prerequisites
Point the exporter at a reachable qBittorrent WebUI. Set either `settings.config.base_url` (e.g. `http://qbittorrent:8080`) or the `settings.config.host`/`port`/`protocol` pieces.

Authentication is optional. To authenticate, set `settings.auth.username` and `settings.auth.password` (mapped to `QBITTORRENT_USERNAME` / `QBITTORRENT_PASSWORD`). When using `settings.auth.existingSecret`, the referenced secret must provide the `QBITTORRENT_USERNAME` and `QBITTORRENT_PASSWORD` keys.

## Install
```bash
helm install qbittorrent-exporter homeylab/qbittorrent-exporter -n qbittorrent-exporter --create-namespace

# with own values file - recommended
helm install -f my-values.yaml qbittorrent-exporter homeylab/qbittorrent-exporter -n qbittorrent-exporter --create-namespace
```

### OCI Registry Support
```bash
helm install qbittorrent-exporter -n qbittorrent-exporter oci://registry-1.docker.io/homeylabcharts/qbittorrent-exporter --version X.Y.Z --create-namespace
```

### Install Example
Only some options are shown, view `values.yaml` for an exhaustive list.

<details closed>
<summary>custom-values.yaml</summary>
<br>

```yaml
# custom-values.yaml
settings:
  config:
    base_url: "http://qbittorrent:8080"
  auth:
    username: "admin"
    password: "fakepassword"

metrics:
  enabled: true
  serviceMonitor:
    enabled: true

## Alternative to ingress - Gateway API HTTPRoute (chart does NOT create the Gateway)
# httproute:
#   enabled: true
#   parentRefs:
#     - name: my-gateway
#       namespace: gateway-system
#   hostnames:
#     - qbittorrent-exporter.example.org
```
</details>
<br>

## Upgrade
```bash
helm upgrade qbittorrent-exporter homeylab/qbittorrent-exporter -n qbittorrent-exporter

# with own values file - recommended
helm upgrade -f my-values.yaml qbittorrent-exporter homeylab/qbittorrent-exporter -n qbittorrent-exporter
```

### Upgrade Matrix For Releases
_The matrix below displays certain versions of this helm chart that could result in breaking changes._

| Start Chart Version | Target Chart Version | Upgrade Steps |
| ------------------- | -------------------- | ------------- |
| `0.1.X` | `1.0.0` | Hardened `securityContext` defaults and a standardized image schema now apply; credentials move into a chart-managed Secret (behavior-preserving). **Read the [Migrating to 1.0.0](#migrating-to-100) section in full before upgrading.** |

## Migrating to 1.0.0
First major release. Most changes are transparent if you already set `settings.auth.username`/`password` — review the breaking items below before upgrading.

- **Hardened `securityContext` defaults (breaking — motivates the major bump).** The pod now runs with `runAsNonRoot`, `runAsUser: 65534`, `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`, and `drop: [ALL]`. Because the upstream image is a JVM (`openjdk:17`), the chart also mounts a writable `/tmp` emptyDir for `java.io.tmpdir`. Override `podSecurityContext`/`securityContext` if you run a customized image.
- **Credentials render into a chart-managed Secret (behavior-preserving).** `settings.auth.username`/`password` now populate `templates/secret.yaml`, wired via `envFrom`. The container still receives the same `QBITTORRENT_USERNAME`/`QBITTORRENT_PASSWORD`, so no action is needed. `settings.auth.existingSecret` users are unaffected.
- **Image schema standardized (breaking).** The image block moved to `image.registry`/`image.repository`/`image.tag`; the new `registry` field defaults to `docker.io` (no image change). The post-install test image renamed from `testCurlImage` (`repository`/`name`) to `testConnImage` (`registry`/`repository`/`tag`) — adjust only if you overrode those fields.
- **New optional Ingress (`networking.k8s.io/v1`) and Gateway API `HTTPRoute`.** Both disabled by default; enable with `ingress.enabled` or `httproute.enabled` (the chart does not create the Gateway).
- **TCP liveness/readiness probes** on the metrics port are now set by default (the exporter exposes only `/metrics`, so HTTP health probes are not used).

## Configuration Options
| Key | Type | Default | Description |
|-----|------|---------|-------------|
| affinity | object | `{}` |  |
| extraEnv | object | `{}` | Add extra environment variables |
| fullnameOverride | string | `""` |  |
| httproute.annotations | object | `{}` | annotations added to the HTTPRoute |
| httproute.enabled | bool | `false` | enable a Gateway API (gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1) HTTPRoute as an alternative to ingress |
| httproute.hostnames | list | `[]` | hostnames matched by the route |
| httproute.parentRefs | list | `[]` | parentRefs pointing at the Gateway(s) to attach to; `name` is required |
| httproute.rules | list | `[]` | explicit HTTPRoute rules; if empty, a single rule routing all paths to the Service is generated |
| image.pullPolicy | string | `"IfNotPresent"` | image pull policy |
| image.registry | string | `"docker.io"` | image registry |
| image.repository | string | `"caseyscarborough/qbittorrent-exporter"` | image repository |
| image.tag | string | `""` | overrides the image tag whose default is the chart appVersion |
| imagePullSecrets | list | `[]` |  |
| ingress.annotations | object | `{}` |  |
| ingress.className | string | `""` |  |
| ingress.enabled | bool | `false` |  |
| ingress.hosts[0].host | string | `"chart-example.local"` |  |
| ingress.hosts[0].paths[0].path | string | `"/"` |  |
| ingress.hosts[0].paths[0].pathType | string | `"ImplementationSpecific"` |  |
| ingress.tls | list | `[]` |  |
| livenessProbe.failureThreshold | int | `5` |  |
| livenessProbe.initialDelaySeconds | int | `10` |  |
| livenessProbe.periodSeconds | int | `15` |  |
| livenessProbe.successThreshold | int | `1` |  |
| livenessProbe.tcpSocket.port | string | `"metrics"` |  |
| livenessProbe.timeoutSeconds | int | `3` |  |
| metrics.enabled | bool | `true` | enable/disable prometheus podAnnotations and serviceMonitor |
| metrics.podAnnotations | object | `{"prometheus.io/path":"/metrics","prometheus.io/port":"17871","prometheus.io/scrape":"true"}` | Add podAnnotations for prometheus scraping |
| metrics.podAnnotations."prometheus.io/path" | string | `"/metrics"` | set the path for prometheus scraping |
| metrics.podAnnotations."prometheus.io/port" | string | `"17871"` | set the port for prometheus scraping, should match the service port |
| metrics.prometheusRule.enabled | bool | `false` | enable/disable prometheusRule |
| metrics.prometheusRule.labels | object | `{}` | set labels for prometheusRule |
| metrics.prometheusRule.rules | list | `[]` | Configure prometheusRule rules for the chart |
| metrics.serviceMonitor.additionalLabels | object | `{}` | set additional labels for serviceMonitor |
| metrics.serviceMonitor.enabled | bool | `false` | enable/disable serviceMonitor, if enabled podAnnotations will be ignored |
| metrics.serviceMonitor.interval | string | `"2m"` | how often to scrape for serviceMonitor |
| metrics.serviceMonitor.metricRelabelings | list | `[]` |  |
| metrics.serviceMonitor.path | string | `"/metrics"` | set the path for prometheus scraping for serviceMonitor |
| metrics.serviceMonitor.relabelings | list | `[]` |  |
| metrics.serviceMonitor.scrapeTimeout | string | `"30s"` | scrapeTimeout for serviceMonitor |
| metrics.serviceMonitor.targetLabels | list | `[]` |  |
| nameOverride | string | `""` |  |
| nodeSelector | object | `{}` |  |
| podAnnotations | object | `{}` |  |
| podLabels | object | `{}` |  |
| podSecurityContext.fsGroup | int | `65534` |  |
| podSecurityContext.runAsNonRoot | bool | `true` |  |
| podSecurityContext.runAsUser | int | `65534` |  |
| podSecurityContext.seccompProfile.type | string | `"RuntimeDefault"` |  |
| readinessProbe.failureThreshold | int | `5` |  |
| readinessProbe.initialDelaySeconds | int | `10` |  |
| readinessProbe.periodSeconds | int | `15` |  |
| readinessProbe.successThreshold | int | `1` |  |
| readinessProbe.tcpSocket.port | string | `"metrics"` |  |
| readinessProbe.timeoutSeconds | int | `3` |  |
| resources | object | `{}` |  |
| securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation | bool | `false` |  |
| securityContext.capabilities.drop[0] | string | `"ALL"` |  |
| securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem | bool | `true` |  |
| securityContext.runAsNonRoot | bool | `true` |  |
| service.name | string | `"metrics"` | set name for the service port (referenced by probes and serviceMonitor) |
| service.port | int | `17871` | set port for qbittorrent-exporter scraping (upstream default 17871) |
| service.protocol | string | `"TCP"` | set protocol for the service |
| service.type | string | `"ClusterIP"` |  |
| serviceAccount.annotations | object | `{}` |  |
| serviceAccount.automount | bool | `true` |  |
| serviceAccount.create | bool | `false` |  |
| serviceAccount.name | string | `""` |  |
| settings.auth | object | `{"existingSecret":"","password":"","username":""}` | Set auth for qBittorrent. If no auth provided, the exporter runs unauthenticated |
| settings.auth.existingSecret | string | `""` | use an existing secret instead of `auth.username`/`auth.password`. The secret must provide the `QBITTORRENT_USERNAME` and `QBITTORRENT_PASSWORD` keys |
| settings.auth.password | string | `""` | qBittorrent WebUI password. Maps to `QBITTORRENT_PASSWORD` |
| settings.auth.username | string | `""` | qBittorrent WebUI username. Maps to `QBITTORRENT_USERNAME` |
| settings.config | object | `{"base_url":"","host":"","port":"","protocol":"http"}` | Add configuration as environment variables |
| settings.config.base_url | string | `""` | full qBittorrent base URL, e.g. `http://somehost:8080` or `https://qbit.example.org`. If set, host/port/protocol are ignored |
| settings.config.host | string | `""` | qBittorrent host (used when base_url is empty) |
| settings.config.port | string | `""` | qBittorrent port (used when base_url is empty) |
| settings.config.protocol | string | `"http"` | qBittorrent protocol (used when base_url is empty) |
| testConnImage.path | string | `"/metrics"` | the path for the post-install connection check |
| testConnImage.pullPolicy | string | `"IfNotPresent"` | test image pull policy |
| testConnImage.registry | string | `"docker.io"` | test image registry |
| testConnImage.repository | string | `"busybox"` | test image repository |
| testConnImage.tag | string | `"1.36.1"` | test image tag |
| tolerations | list | `[]` |  |
| updateStrategy.type | string | `"Recreate"` | set the update strategy for the deployment |
| volumeMounts | list | `[]` |  |
| volumes | list | `[]` |  |
